---
title: "Logic Matters"
page-layout: article
title-block-banner: true
---


**[THE FIVE BIG RED LOGIC BOOKS](books)** The headline news — they are open access.

**[LOGIC MATTERS BLOG](blog)** Peter Smith’s occasional blog posts on logic, music, and other enthusiasms.

{{< include _recent_posts.md >}}

**[INTRO TO FORMAL LOGIC](ifl)**. A corrected version of the second edition of *An Introduction to Formal Logic* is freely available here. There are also links to answers to exercises and to various supplementary materials.

**[GÖDEL’S THEOREMS](igt)**. Two books are freely available here, *An Introduction to Gödel’s Theorems* and the much shorter *Gödel Without (Too Many) Tears*, along with related materials.

**[CATEGORY THEORY](categories)** Links to resources at an introductory/intermediate level, such as lecture notes and freely available books, including my *Introducing Category Theory*.

**[THE STUDY GUIDE AND BOOK NOTES](tyl)** You will find here the much-downloaded *Beginning Mathematical Logic: A Study Guide*, its *Appendix* reviewing some of the wider-ranging books on mathematical logic, together with links to some forty further book notes.

**[ARCHIVE](https://www.logicmatters.net/yet-more-logic)** Links to various old blog posts, papers, more reviews and other pieces.

**[LaTeX FOR LOGICIANS](https://www.logicmatters.net/latex-for-logicians)** Pages pointing to a wide range of resources of interest to logicians, philosophers and others using LaTeX.
